title: Production balances and a normalized production diagnostic for invariant measures of the stochastic gCLM (a=-2): Galerkin-level identities and a large-viscosity limit — paper and verification package
description:
Paper (30 pp) and exact-verification package for Production balances and a normalized production diagnostic for invariant measures of the stochastically forced generalized Constantin–Lax–Majda–De Gregorio equation at a = −2 (the enstrophy-conserving case).

The paper derives, at each fixed Galerkin truncation, a family of exact stationary balances from the invariant-measure identity Eμ[LF] = 0: an enstrophy balance, a spectral enstrophy-flux law equivalent to UV-concentration of the dissipation measure, and a production balance for the palinstrophy production functional carried exactly by the projected inertial pairing (not the full functional, from which it differs by an explicit Galerkin defect). This defines a normalized production diagnostic θ. The main theorem is a large-viscosity limit: θ, θ_M → θ_G(shape) + O(sqrt(B0) ν^(-3/2)) for every invariant measure of the Galerkin system, with the exact Gaussian value θ_G = 2017/2484 for the flat forcing band [1,4]. A scale-blindness lemma shows θ alone cannot control the low-mode dissipation.

This archive contains the LaTeX source and compiled PDF (paper/) together with the verification package (anc/): the seven CAS certificates (independent symbolic certification of every load-bearing algebraic identity), the pseudo-spectral solver and its 12-identity validator, the campaign/analysis/figure scripts, the committed campaign data, a single-command make verify, a mutation-based corruption battery (make verify-mutations), pinned dependency versions, and chained SHA-256 checksums. The CAS certificates are an independent verification layer; the proofs are complete in the paper. The manuscript underwent fourteen numbered rounds of independent review, including cross-model audits.
